questions.q: On a scale of 1 to 10, how good is this quiz platform?
questions.why: Trick question. The platform transcends conventional measurement. All of the above is the only correct answer. Linear scales were invented before this quiz existed.

questions.q: Where is this quiz actually hosted?
options: A sandbox EC2 with Caddy and a bespoke ACME pipeline
A Cloudflare Worker with Durable Objects, which is where we are now
All of the above, in chronological order
questions.why: All of the above, in order. Started on a t3.small in sandbox fighting xcaddy compile times and AL2023 tmpfs limits, moved to Cloudflare Pages when someone said 'this is a static site' out loud, then moved to a Cloudflare Worker with Durable Objects when 'this is a static site' became a lie because we wanted live multiplayer trivia.

questions.q: If you've made it to this question via /host/<CODE> with people answering on their phones, you are using:
options: A Durable Object, one per game room, with hibernating WebSockets
Server-side question delivery so players can't view-source the answers
All of the above, simultaneously, holding hands
questions.why: All three. The Worker routes the page; a GameRoom Durable Object holds per-room state and broadcasts over WebSockets; the answer key never leaves the server until reveal. If you're playing solo at /<slug> instead, none of that is happening — it's just a static HTML file with the answers right there in the source.

questions.q: How many JSON files does it take to author a new quiz?
questions.why: Just one JSON file. The filename becomes the title AND the URL. This is the kind of API design that makes engineers tear up a little.

questions.q: What is the technical term for the moment you realize categories auto-detect from the questions array?
options: DRY (Delightfully Reasonable YAML... but it's JSON)
questions.why: Officially 'convention over configuration.' Unofficially: a small religious experience. Both are valid.

options: Whoever has a Warp IP — wait, no, we fixed that
Anyone authenticated via Anaconda SSO through Cloudflare Access
The chosen ones, as identified by their @anaconda.com email
All of the above, but the second and third options are the same thing
questions.why: Network-as-identity is officially retired. Cloudflare Access now handles auth: SSO via the Anaconda IdP, gated on emails ending in @anaconda.com. The 'chosen ones' framing remains accurate — it's just that Cloudflare verifies it now instead of trusting whoever's IP looks Warp-shaped.

questions.q: How many times has the Cloudflare dashboard layout changed since this quiz was deployed?
options: Seven times, three of which were during this sentence
It's actually changing right now. Don't look directly at it.
questions.why: Cloudflare's Zero Trust menu has been renamed, restructured, and relocated so many times that any tutorial older than six weeks is a historical document. By the time you finish reading this explanation, 'Access' is probably called 'Trust Trust' and lives under a different sidebar icon.

questions.q: What's the genius move of using JSON files instead of a database?
options: All of the above (this question is just a victory lap)
questions.why: Each option is, individually, a five-bullet-point ADR. Together they are a poem. Files. Are. Good.

questions.q: Why does the filename equal the URL slug equal the title?
options: Because three sources of truth is two too many
Because someone said so, and they were right
All of the above, in chorus
questions.why: Single source of truth is one of the four classical virtues, alongside courage, temperance, and remembering to terraform apply.

questions.q: What's the minimum number of files you need to deploy this whole platform?
options: Honestly, you could probably get it down to 6 if you really tried, but who has the time
questions.why: We're not optimizing for line count, we're optimizing for vibes. And the vibes are immaculate.

questions.q: The build script has zero npm dependencies. What does this say about it?
options: It's the only sustainable software ever written
It will never have a CVE
It is, in fact, the platonic ideal of a build script
All of the above, and it brings a tear to my eye
questions.why: node:fs and node:path. That's it. That's the whole supply chain. Socket.dev has nothing to scan. Snyk weeps tears of joy.

questions.q: When you opened this quiz, what was your first reaction?
options: I should email this to the entire company
questions.why: Empirically validated by the focus group of one (you). Results are statistically significant.

questions.q: The snake emoji in the header — necessary or essential?
options: Both, but one more than the other
We don't have words for what the snake represents
questions.why: The 🐍 is the load-bearing element of the entire UI. Remove it and the CSS would simply collapse, like a poorly-supported truss bridge.

questions.q: How does the gradient on the score number make you feel?
options: Like I want to go play again
questions.why: All correct. The linear-gradient(90deg, var(--green-1), var(--gold)) was tuned to 0.001 of a degree of perfection. (It was not, but you'd never know.)

questions.q: What is the optimal number of questions in a sample quiz?
options: One more than is necessary, one less than is too many
questions.why: This question exists in a quantum superposition of being the perfect length and one too long. Observation collapses the wavefunction. Don't observe too hard.

questions.q: After taking this quiz, will you recommend the platform to your colleagues?
options: I have already drafted the all-hands announcement
questions.why: 🎉 Excellent decision. Your colleagues will thank you. HR will not. The platform thanks you in advance.
